← All worlds/World 02
World 02 · 2 states

Loneliness

Films for the silence that lives between you and everyone else — and the strange comfort of knowing that silence is not unique to you.

Choose an emotion
Quiet IsolationFeeling Invisible
On Feeling Invisible

You have spent years in rooms where people looked slightly past you — close enough that they might have seen you, far enough that you could tell they did not. You became very good at watching. You learned everyone else's patterns. You made yourself smaller so the absence would hurt less. These films were made for you. Not the version of you performing confidence, but the one who sat at the edge of the lunch table trying to look like sitting there was a choice. These stories see that kid. They always did.

Feeling Invisible

Films for the kid at the back of every room.

CS8.6
2012

The Perks of Being a Wallflower

Stephen Chbosky

View Film →

A shy, brilliant freshman with a complicated past is taken in by two senior misfits and slowly learns what it means to be chosen.

CS9.6
2016

Moonlight

Barry Jenkins

View Film →

A young black man’s life in Miami across three chapters — childhood, teenage years, and adulthood — as he learns who he is and who he is allowed to love.

CS8.4
2022

The Whale

Darren Aronofsky

View Film →

A reclusive English teacher trying to reconnect with his estranged daughter in the final week of his life, told mostly inside a single apartment.

CS8.4
2019

Joker

Todd Phillips

View Film →

A struggling clown and aspiring stand-up comic in a decaying Gotham City descends, step by step, into a man the world made out of indifference.

CS9.0
2017

Lady Bird

Greta Gerwig

View Film →

A senior at a Catholic high school in Sacramento clashes with her mother, dreams of New York, and tries to figure out which version of herself is the real one.

CS8.6
2012

Frances Ha

Noah Baumbach

View Film →

A twenty-seven-year-old aspiring dancer in New York drifts between apartments, friendships, and ideas of herself, trying to land somewhere.

CS8.9
2018

Eighth Grade

Bo Burnham

View Film →

A thirteen-year-old YouTuber tries to survive the last week of middle school, armed with too much advice and not enough audience.

CS8.4
2016

The Edge of Seventeen

Kelly Fremon Craig

View Film →

A junior in high school whose best friend starts dating her older brother must figure out who she is when her one anchor moves out of reach.

CS9.0
2009

Mary and Max

Adam Elliot

View Film →

A pen-pal friendship between a lonely Australian girl and a middle-aged New Yorker with autism unfolds across two decades, in claymation, in letters.

CS8.8
2013

Inside Llewyn Davis

Coen Brothers

View Film →

A folk singer in 1961 Greenwich Village shuttles between couches and gigs, talented and unlucky, with a cat that may or may not be his.

CS9.0
2013

Short Term 12

Destin Daniel Cretton

View Film →

A twenty-something supervisor at a foster care facility for at-risk teenagers manages her wards, her boyfriend, and her own unprocessed trauma.

CS8.6
2009

An Education

Lone Scherfig

View Film →

A sixteen-year-old Oxford-track student in 1961 London falls for a charming older man and is briefly convinced education might be the wrong road.

CS8.8
1985

The Breakfast Club

John Hughes

View Film →

Five high school students — athlete, brain, criminal, princess, basket case — serve Saturday detention together and reveal who they actually are.

CS8.2
2018

Mid90s

Jonah Hill

View Film →

A lonely thirteen-year-old in 1990s LA finds belonging in a crew of older skaters. The belonging is real and the dangers are real.

CS8.4
2013

The Spectacular Now

James Ponsoldt

View Film →

A popular but directionless high school senior falls for a quiet, invisible girl and both are changed.

CS8.9
1993

Dazed and Confused

Richard Linklater

View Film →

The last day of high school in 1976 Austin. Freshmen, seniors, weed, hazing, and the slow recognition that these years are almost over.

CS9.0
2000

Almost Famous

Cameron Crowe

View Film →

A fifteen-year-old secretly writes for Rolling Stone in 1973, going on tour with a fictional rock band.

CS8.1
2003

Thirteen

Catherine Hardwicke

View Film →

A thirteen-year-old LA girl falls under the influence of the most dangerous girl in school. Co-written by its then-thirteen-year-old co-star.

CS9.0
2020

Never Rarely Sometimes Always

Eliza Hittman

View Film →

A seventeen-year-old from rural Pennsylvania and her cousin travel to New York to access reproductive healthcare. The system makes them invisible at every step.

CS8.7
2015

Mustang

Deniz Gamze Ergüven

View Film →

Five orphaned sisters in rural Turkey are gradually imprisoned by their uncle after a perceived scandal, their freedoms removed one by one.

CS8.6
2012

Beasts of the Southern Wild

Benh Zeitlin

View Film →

A six-year-old girl in a Louisiana bayou community called the Bathtub faces the flooding of her world and her father’s illness through mythological imagination.

CS8.4
2007

Juno

Jason Reitman

View Film →

A sixteen-year-old navigates an unintended pregnancy with wit, vinyl records, and a deeply examined emotional intelligence.

CS8.0
2013

The Kings of Summer

Jordan Vogt-Roberts

View Film →

Three teenage boys build a house in the woods and run away from their families for a summer.

Films for the version of you no one quite reaches.

Identity Crisis →

For when you don’t know who you are anymore.

Films don't just entertain. They quietly confirm you were seen — long before anyone in your real life knew how to.
